title: poa arctica subsp. williamsii
intro: Poa arctica subsp. williamsii, also known as Williams' Arctic Bluegrass, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the grass family. It is native to high-altitude regions of Alaska and Canada. The medicinal properties of Poa arctica subsp. williamsii include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to alleviate symptoms of fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, while also providing overall health benefits such as immune system support and antioxidant protection.
